name: Support Ticket Classification
description: Classifies support tickets into predefined categories based on the content of the ticket
task_description:
{"prompt_template": "Classify this support ticket into one of these categories:\n- billing\n- technical\n- account\n- general\n\nTicket:\n{ticket_text}\n\nReturn only the category name.", "input": {"ticket_text": "I was charged twice for my subscription this month. Please refund the duplicate charge."}}

**IMPORTANT: The task provides these input variables:**
- `prompt` (string) - **THE RAW INPUT DATA** that varies per instance. Your activity MUST parse and process this!
- `_task_schema` (dict) - context for task type
- `ticket_text` (str) - context for task type

**CRITICAL:** Your workflow MUST use `prompt` as the input variable. The prompt contains the raw data to process.
Available variables: prompt, _task_schema, ticket_text

**OUTPUT FORMAT (STRUCTURE DESCRIPTION - NO HARDCODED VALUES!):**
Your activity must return data matching this structure:
```json
{
  "type": "string",
  "description": "One of: billing, technical, account, general - lowercase category name"
}
```

**CRITICAL RULES:**
- The format above describes FIELD NAMES and TYPES only
- Extract ACTUAL values from the `prompt` variable at runtime
- Do NOT hardcode any values from examples
- Do NOT add extra fields
- Match field names EXACTLY as specified
reasoning: This workflow requires semantic understanding of the ticket content to determine the appropriate category. A single LLM-based classification activity is the most efficient approach since the task requires context understanding rather than pattern matching. The LLM can analyze the meaning and intent behind the ticket text to make accurate category determinations. Using sequence execution pattern since there's only one activity to execute.
